meta.title: MCOO Falkland Islands / Malvinas 1982
meta.perspective: British intelligence staff analyzing Argentine forces

points.name: San Carlos Water / Ajax Bay
points.meaning: British landing area and beachhead; sheltered water but vulnerable to Argentine air attack.

points.name: Port San Carlos
points.meaning: Northern landing and assembly area for UK forces.

points.name: Goose Green / Darwin
points.meaning: Argentine position and local airstrip; important southern flank objective.

points.name: Mount Kent
points.meaning: High ground and observation point on approaches to Stanley.

points.name: Mount Longdon
points.meaning: Argentine defensive high ground north-west of Stanley.

points.name: Wireless Ridge
points.meaning: Argentine defensive ridge linking Mount Longdon to Stanley.

points.name: Mount Tumbledown
points.meaning: Critical southern defensive terrain guarding Stanley.

points.name: Sapper Hill
points.meaning: Final western defensive ground before Stanley.

points.name: Stanley / Port Stanley
points.meaning: Main objective; political, military, port, and administrative center.

points.name: Stanley Airport
points.meaning: Airfield, sustainment, and C2 infrastructure node.

points.name: Fitzroy / Bluff Cove
points.meaning: Southern landing and logistics risk area exposed to air attack.

points.name: Teal Inlet
points.meaning: Route node and assembly point on northern approach to Stanley.

points.name: Douglas Settlement
points.meaning: Route node toward Mount Kent and Two Sisters.

routes.name: AA-1 Port San Carlos - Mount Longdon - Stanley
AA-2 Ajax Bay - Mount Harriet - Stanley
ARG air threat corridor toward San Carlos

areas.name: Slow Go: peat bog / limited roads west-central East Falkland
Slow Go: central East Falkland approaches to Stanley
No Go: Stanley ridge defence / mine risk
Go Corridor: San Carlos to Stanley
